title: systemtest flakes
body:
Not always, but often enough, system tests fails with something like
```
not ok 8 auth: credentials on command line
# (from function `expect_output' in file systemtest/helpers.bash, line 189,
#  in test file systemtest/040-local-registry-auth.bats, line 27)
#   `expect_output --substring "unauthorized: authentication required"' failed
# grep: /tmp/skopeo_bats.WTZxVj/auth/htpasswd: No such file or directory
# 66f1545ce2662b1957fd55ff3ee1540af40fbc10bbbd462caa2e444805ad788b
# $ /usr/bin/skopeo inspect --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/nonesuch
# time="2019-06-13T12:57:38Z" level=fatal msg="pinging docker registry returned: Get http://localhost:5000/v2/: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5000: connect: connection refused" 
# [ rc=1 (expected) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: skopeo inspect --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/nonesuch
# #| expected: 'unauthorized: authentication required'
# #|   actual: 'time="2019-06-13T12:57:38Z" level=fatal msg="pinging docker registry returned: Get http://localhost:5000/v2/: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5000: connect: connection refused" '
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# 66f1545ce2662b1957fd55ff3ee1540af40fbc10bbbd462caa2e444805ad788b
```
(omitting many instances of `level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket""`

Restarting the build usually lets it proceed.

Not sure how to best fix this (or even, for sure, what the cause actually is) – maybe `start_registry` just needs to wait for the registry to start up (some random time, or until the port is known to be working).

Cc: @edsantiago
